summary: Full E2E test of NEW admin capability: gift/revoke premium and cascade-delete users. 17 new backend tests + full UI E2E all PASS. Regressions (auth, admin, abbonamento) all green.

covered: grant-premium lifetime -> premium_until starts with 2099 and target /subscription/status.premium=true
/admin/users row includes premium (bool) + premium_until, respected round-trip
DELETE /admin/users/{self} -> 400 'Non puoi eliminare il tuo account'
DELETE normal user -> 200; user gone from /admin/users; pets/visits/vaccines/treatments/weights all cascaded in Mongo; target session dead (401 on /me)

frontend_test_details.test_flow_summary: Registered throwaway test_ui_{ts}@example.com via /api/auth/register, cleared cookies, logged in via UI as admin@pawcare.it, went to /admin, verified row/badge state through 4 lifecycle transitions (Gratuito -> monthly -> lifetime -> Gratuito -> deleted). Real accounts admin@pawcare.it and teresa.compi98@gmail.com left untouched (visible in final screenshot).

action_items: OPTIONAL cleanup: DB currently holds ~10 leftover test_* accounts from prior iterations (test_role8_*, test_gift_*, test_008c9b12, test_b8e81bd5, etc.). Consider a cleanup script or admin bulk-delete since PawCare only has one real user besides admin (teresa.compi98@gmail.com).
OPTIONAL UX: The 'Utenti Premium' stat card icon is a Crown; consider a distinct color/accent to visually differentiate from other cards (currently uses same text-primary as others). Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_grant_premium 'lifetime' branch inlines the write (bypasses _grant_premium). Fine — but it forcibly overwrites premium_until (no extension). This is intentional for lifetime.

_cascade_delete_user is correct: deletes pets first (collects pet_ids), then cascades visits/vaccines/treatments/weights/documents by pet_id, then wipes user-scoped collections (chat, ai_usage, payments, push, sessions). Order and idempotency look good.
Admin.jsx correctly hides the 'Elimina account' MenuItem when u.role === 'admin' — dropdown for admin user has zero delete items. Defense-in-depth: backend also returns 400 if attempted.

premiumLabel uses u.premium_until.startsWith('2099') to detect lifetime. Works because backend always stores ISO 2099-01-01. If lifetime year ever changes this breaks — consider a boolean flag u.lifetime instead.

Backend /admin/users iterates users and does N+3 queries per user (pet_count, chat_count, has_push, is_premium is in-memory only). Fine for admin panel with hundreds of users; would need aggregation pipeline at scale.
server.py is now 1106 lines — approaching the 700-line split threshold. Consider splitting into routes/admin.py, routes/pets.py, routes/subscription.py.
seed_data_creation: One throwaway user per test method (test_gift_*/test_ui_*). All test_gift_* users created for tests were left behind by pytest since no teardown fixture. The single UI test user was DELETED via the UI flow. Real accounts (admin@pawcare.it, teresa.compi98@gmail.com) were NEVER touched — confirmed by presence in final screenshot.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin premium mgmt (gift/revoke/delete) is fully functional end-to-end. Data-testids in place: user-actions-{uid}, gift-monthly, delete-user, confirm-delete-user, admin-stats, admin-users-table. TestAdminPremiumManagement class covers all edge cases (self-delete 400, other-admin 400, unknown 404, auth gates 401/403, cascade delete verified via direct pymongo). Leftover test_* accounts pile up in DB — main agent might want a periodic cleanup job or 'bulk delete test users' admin action.
